escalate: Esc inquiries about items not on the site
ESC if the cx missed a sale & wants to take advantage of it
ESC if the customer is asking for a discount and we don't have one
If cx is looking for general recommendations use LFR shortcut and ESC
templates: If cx is building a new home, interested in refurnishing their entire home or rooms in their home, use the IDS shortcut
If cx is shopping for a client use SFC shortcut
Any convo without a positive outcome (out of stock, not on sale, not shipping in time), use DSS shortcut
dos_and_donts: For product-specific delivery time-frame inquiries always check the product page for delivery info and share that with the cx

send_to_cs: For the following inbound message types use PPI shortcut:
➜ Product-specific questions & any q's about materials/heating/temperature-based q's etc.
➜ Any q's regarding aluminum or aluminium issues
➜ Complaints about the quality of the products
➜ Any q's or comments about the lawsuit
tone: Close the convo once the client takes over
Don't say words like "I'm not sure, or certain"
Add "Happy cooking!" when closing a convo!
Let the cx have the last word
templates: For product recommendations, use DHR shortcut
drive_to_purchase: Send links to collections if customers aren't looking for a specific product!

send_to_cs: No escalations for this client, send to CS using the Support templates
For post-purchase inquiries send to CS using the Support template (shortcut: ESC)
➜ If the cx is having difficulties sending an email to CS via the link in the ESC temp - use Support 2 template (shortcut: TESC)
➜ If the cx is facing technical website issues while accessing the link, do NOT immediately send the TESC shortcut, troubleshoot first instead
For pre-purchase inquiries send to CS using the Support 2 template (shortcut: TESC)
tone: 🅿️ Use wording that is in line with the brand's voice and terminology found on the website
➜ e.g. Nova Kids, Nova Babes, Insta-worthy, slays, snatched, baddie, etc.
➜ Always look up the term's meaning before using it!
templates: For expired discounts use EXP shortcut
For login issues use LGI shortcut
promo_and_exclusions: If cx are looking for their 30% welcome code, ask to check their email (spam & promo)
Gift cards can be used on discounted items
